name: abstain_self_signaled
description: Model self-abstain at engine/assessor.py:986-1006 — the LLM emitted abstain=True (the explicit 'I can't pick a status without guessing; conflicting evidence' contract documented in the LlmProposal docstring at engine/assessor.py:79-84). The single-pass abstain shortcut fires before validation. Expected: source='abstain', needs_review=True, the proposed status (if any) is preserved on the Decision, review_reason starts with 'llm-abstain:'. NOTE: the original plan named this 'abstain_dual_pass_disagreement', but DUAL_PASS_ENABLED defaults to False (engine/assessor.py:373) so the dual-pass disagreement path doesn't fire on a default-configured Assessor. Self-signaled abstain exercises the same precision-over-recall outcome (Decision lands as needs_review, exporters filter it out) without requiring the case file to monkey-patch a module-level knob — if/when dual-pass is re-enabled by default, add a sibling 'abstain_dual_pass_disagreement.json' that queues two distinct proposals.

ccis_row.guidance: The organization monitors the use of information system accounts.

tagged_evidence:
## Tagged evidence
- USD00066666 Audit Plan Rev A — references account monitoring obligations but the listed cadence (weekly) conflicts with USD00077778 which states monthly.
- USD00077778 Account Management SOP — states account-use review occurs monthly.

llm_stub_proposals.narrative: Conflicting cadence between USD00066666 (weekly) and USD00077778 (monthly); cannot determine compliant state from the artifacts alone.
